# Engine levers — the complete command surface

*Generated by tools/gen_levers.py from the desktop engine's
generated manifests. Do not edit by hand; regenerate.*

The iOS shell (and any Claude design workflow building screens)
drives the engine exclusively through `pms_command(json) -> json`.
These are the commands. If a screen needs a lever that isn't
here, that's an engine change (add handler, regen), never a
bypass into engine internals.

## Commands (83)

- **`add_audio_multifx_brick`** — Add an Audio Multi-FX brick: an ordered chain of LIVE audio effects that auto-couples (1.5s weld timer skipped â couples immediately) to the best-overlapping 
- **`add_body_fx_brick`** — Add a Body FX solid brick to a track. The brick applies a body/silhouette-based visual effect (e.g. neon outline, depth blur, glitch, retro TV) to the composite
- **`add_callout`** — Add a callout text overlay â a positioned text box with optional arrow pointing at a canvas location. Good for labelling objects, people, or actions on screen
- **`add_chapter_marker`** — Add a chapter/section marker at a specific timeline position. Markers appear as colored vertical lines with labels in the timeline ruler. They are sorted by tim
- **`add_clip`** — Place a clip on a track.
- **`add_clip_sequence`** — Add multiple clips to a single track in one round-trip. Equivalent to calling add_clip N times but dramatically faster for beat-sync edits or any bulk layout. R
- **`add_effect_brick`** — Add a standalone FX brick to a track. Affects everything below on the timeline (or only the sibling video clip if on the same track â glass mode).
- **`add_multifx_brick`** — Add an ordered chain of FX sub-effects as one brick. This is THE way to layer effects over the same time range: FX bricks cannot overlap each other on a track (
- **`add_to_bin`** — Add a media file to the project Bin. The Bin is the project-scoped media library shown in the right-side panel â files here are 'available to use' but are not
- **`add_track`** — Add a track at position (0=top/foreground, higher=background). Returns track index.
- **`analyze_audio`** — Run beat/RMS analysis on an audio file. Blocks until complete â returns {status: 'done', bpm, duration, beats, rms} when finished. No polling needed.
- **`apply_multicam_cuts`** — Given multiple camera-angle clips on parallel tracks, apply a cut list that keeps only the selected camera in each time window. All other camera-track clips in 
- **`begin_batch`** — Group multiple mutations into one undo step.
- **`cancel_export`** — Cancel a running export. No batch needed.
- **`cancel_search`** — Cancel a running find_and_add_clip / search_transcript operation. Safe to call even if no search is running. No batch needed.
- **`collect_project`** — Save the project as a self-contained folder: copy every referenced media original (clip sources, LUTs, master audio, bin) into a media/ folder beside the .pms a
- **`crop_media`** — Reframe a video/image to a target aspect ratio. NON-DESTRUCTIVE by default: computes the crop box (with face detection) and applies it as the clip's crop_l/crop
- **`cut_at_phrase`** — Trim a clip to end (or start) right at a phrase. Collapses transcript lookup + trim into one call.
- **`cut_filler_words`** — Remove filler words ('um', 'uh', 'like', etc.) from a clip using the word-level transcript. Run trigger_pipeline and wait for it to finish first. Use dry_run=tr
- **`decouple_fx_brick`** — Decouple a welded (coupled) Multi-FX brick from its host content clip, turning it back into a free-floating glass brick at its current position. The UI equivale
- **`delete_clip`** — Delete a clip.
- **`delete_clips_after`** — Delete all clips on one track whose start >= time. One undo step. No batch needed.
- **`delete_track`** — Delete a whole track (and all its clips) by index or name. One undo step. No batch needed. To remove individual clips use delete_clip / delete_clips_after inste
- **`describe_video`** — Start local scene analysis: extract keyframes at scene changes and caption each with the on-device Moondream2 model. Returns {status:'started'}, or {status:'cac
- **`detect_screen_activity`** — Scan a screen recording for visual activity, for planning retimes/cuts of technical footage. Runs server-side over the existing .pms_proxy.mjpeg + .idx seek tab
- **`end_batch`** — Commit the current edit batch to the undo history.
- **`find_and_add_clip`** — Find a specific spoken moment in a media file and add it to the timeline. Does windowed speech-to-text search â chunk size is calculated from the file length 
- **`find_audio_cue`** — Find a beat-aligned timestamp matching a description ('first drop', 'chorus', 'outro', etc.).
- **`find_video_moment`** — Find the best-matching moment in a video by visual content. Scores the scene captions from describe_video against the query â run describe_video first (this t
- **`generate_chapters`** — Auto-generate chapter markers from the transcript by finding natural pause points. Call get_transcript first â this tool requires a ready transcript.
- **`get_activity_status`** — Poll a detect_screen_activity job: {state: 'running'|'done'|'error', progress 0-1, result?} â result carries the per-region segment lists when done. Pass the 
- **`get_all_clips`** — Return clips across ALL tracks: [{index, name, clips: [{index, type, start, end, duration, in_point, source, text}]}]. Use for full-timeline orientation without
- **`get_audio_analysis`** — Poll beat/RMS analysis started by analyze_audio. Returns {status: 'idle'|'running'|'done'|'error', bpm?, duration?, beats?, rms?}. Poll every 2s until status='d
- **`get_audio_perf`** — Audio engine performance/latency readout: backend (pipewire native vs SDL), block size, measured input/output latency, xrun count, performance-mode state. Use w
- **`get_chapter_markers`** — Return all timeline markers (a.k.a. chapter/section markers): [{index, time, label, color}], sorted by time. Read-only â no batch needed. Pairs with add_chapt
- **`get_clips`** — Return the clips on one track: [{index, type, start, end, duration, in_point, source, text}]. Use this instead of get_project when you only need clip positions 
- **`get_export_status`** — Poll export progress. Returns {running, progress (0â1), frame, total_frames, eta_secs, stage, output}. Read-only â no batch needed.
- **`get_face_track`** — Live face tracker state (camera mirror): lock validity, detector score, key landmark positions, worker flip/redetect counters, and (full:true) all 106 landmarks
- **`get_media_info`** — Probe a media file and return its codec/format metadata: duration (seconds), width, height, fps, has_video, has_audio, video_codec, audio_codec, sample_rate, ch
- **`get_pipeline_status`** — Returns the current ML pipeline status: stage (idle/extract/transcribe/align/done/error), progress (0â1), message, and error string. trigger_pipeline returns 
- **`get_project`** — Returns project state. Slim by default: {duration, fps, bpm, audio_path, project_path, transcript_ready, playhead, tracks: [{index, name, muted, locked, clip_co
- **`get_search_status`** — Poll a running transcript search started by find_and_add_clip. Returns: running (bool), progress (0â1), message, found (bool), start (seconds), end (seconds),
- **`get_stills`** — Generate and return thumbnail images for a list of media files so you can visually identify their content before deciding which to add to the timeline.
- **`get_transcript`** — Return the word-level transcript produced by the transcription pipeline. Returns {status: 'idle'|'ready'|'error', words?: [{word, start, end}], path?}. Word tim
- **`get_video_description`** — Poll scene analysis started by describe_video (blocks internally until done â call ONCE, not in a loop). Single run â {status, frames:[{timestamp, descripti
- **`list_dir`** — List the files and subfolders in a directory. Use this to DISCOVER media on disk instead of guessing paths or probing files one by one. Accepts a folder path, O
- **`load_project`** — Load a .pms project file, replacing the current project.
- **`make_contact_sheet`** — One tiled thumbnail grid with burned-in timestamps for a list of timeline times on a source file, returned inline â visually verify ambiguous segment boundari
- **`make_section`** — Crop the whole project to a [start, end] window and shift it to begin at 0 â the one-call way to turn a full song into just a section. Clips fully outside the
- **`move_clip`** — Move a clip to a new start time (preserves duration). Moving an FX brick onto another FX brick's time range on the same track is rejected â FX bricks never ov
- **`pause`** — Pause playback.
- **`play`** — Start playback from the current playhead position.
- **`process_body_fx_masks`** — Start body mask processing for a Body FX brick. This analyses the video clip(s) on tracks below the brick and writes body-segmentation masks for the brick's tim
- **`read_transcript_context`** — Read words from the saved transcript for a source video file around a specific timestamp. Use after find_and_add_clip to find exact speaker boundaries, sentence
- **`redo`** — Redo the last undone edit. Returns updated project state. Read-only â no batch needed.
- **`remove_background`** — Remove the background from a video clip using RobustVideoMatting (RVM) alpha matting. Adds a 'Remove Background' body_fx brick on the same track as the video cl
- **`remove_chapter_marker`** — Remove a chapter marker by its index (from get_project markers array or add_chapter_marker response).
- **`remove_from_bin`** — Remove a media file from the project Bin. Does NOT remove clips already placed on the timeline that reference the file â those keep working (the timeline hold
- **`remove_silence`** — Automatically detect and remove silent segments from a video or audio clip. Uses per-second RMS energy (1-second resolution â pauses shorter than ~0.5s may no
- **`rename_track`** — Rename a track.
- **`save_project`** — Save the project to disk as a .pms file. Always use the .pms extension â the UI file picker filters by *.pms, so any other extension (.pmsproj, .json, etc.) p
- **`search_transcript`** — Search the transcript for ALL occurrences of a phrase. Returns every match with source-file timestamps â use this before any phrase-based cut to see if the ph
- **`seek`** — Move the playhead to a time (seconds). Read-only â no batch needed.
- **`select_clip`** — Set the canvas selection â the clip whose transform handles (move/resize/rotate box) show on the preview. Pairs with get_canvas_geometry + ui_input. Omit 'cli
- **`set_audio_path`** — Set the project's master audio source (the file used for transcription and lyric alignment). Normally set automatically when you add the first audio clip â on
- **`set_clip_fx`** — Set parameters on an FX brick attached to a clip. Use after adding an effect (add_effect_brick / add_multifx_brick) to tune it. amount is the master 0â1 stren
- **`set_clip_keyframes`** — Replace the keyframe track for one animatable property on a clip â this is how you animate position, scale, rotation, opacity, volume, pan, or a video FX bric
- **`set_clip_prop`** — Set one property on a clip.
- **`set_clip_props`** — Set properties on multiple clips in one call. ops: [{track, clip, prop, value}]. Setting speed retimes like the UI (clip width rescales; see set_clip_prop) â 
- **`set_format`** — Set the project canvas format / aspect ratio. Three presets:
- **`set_loop_region`** — Arm the transport loop and optionally set the loop brace region. Playback then cycles seamlessly over [start, end] (or the whole timeline if start/end are omitt
- **`set_text_style`** — Set visual text styling on a clip. All fields are optional â only provided fields are updated. Color arrays are [r, g, b, a] with values 0â1.
- **`set_transcript`** — Install a custom word-level transcript for the project's current audio. Writes the canonical cache JSON next to the audio file and reloads the in-memory word ca
- **`set_typography_preset`** — ASSIGN a typography preset to the project's managed lyric bricks. The per-word/phrase lyric bricks come from trigger_pipeline (they live on a managed 'Lyrics' t
- **`split_clip`** — Split a clip at one or more time points. Pass times=[...] to make every cut in one call â never loop split_clip for a multi-cut edit. Returns left_clip/right_
- **`take_snapshot`** — Renders the canvas frame to PNG and returns the image inline so you can see it. Pass an optional time (seconds) to snap at that timestamp without seeking first.
- **`trigger_export`** — Render and export the project to MP4 (or GIF). Blocks until complete â returns {done, success, output, stage} when finished. No batch needed.
- **`trigger_pipeline`** — Kick off the ML pipeline (MDX-Net + transcription) on the project audio. NON-BLOCKING: returns immediately with stage='running'. Poll get_pipeline_status every 
- **`trim_all_to`** — Trim every clip on every track so nothing extends past 'time'. Clips starting at or after 'time' are deleted; clips straddling 'time' are clamped. One undo step
- **`trim_clip`** — Trim a clip's start and/or end time. Trimming an FX brick so it would lap another FX brick on the same track is rejected â FX bricks never overlap.
- **`undo`** — Undo the last edit. Returns updated project state so you can verify the revert. Read-only â no batch needed.
- **`validate_glsl`** — Test-compile a GLSL fragment shader body without registering it as a runtime effect. Returns {ok: bool, error?: string}. Useful before writing a shader to disk.
- **`verify_clips`** — Seek to each time and snapshot to visually confirm clip content. Use after placing video clips from transcript timestamps â pass the midpoint of each clip. Re

## Effects (109) — via `add_effect_brick` / `set_clip_fx`

- **Pixelate** (`pixelate`, Glitch) — `size` [1.0–64.0] (default 12.0)
- **Film Grain** (`film_grain`, Film) — `intensity` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.4), `size` [0.5–4.0] (default 1.0)
- **Scanlines** (`scanlines`, Pattern) — `density` [1.0–6.0] (default 2.0)
- **Chromatic Aberration** (`chromatic_aberration`, Glitch) — amount only
- **Duotone** (`duotone`, Color) — `shadow_r` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.05), `shadow_g` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.02), `shadow_b` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.2), `highlight_r` [0.0–1.0] (default 1.0), `highlight_g` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.85), `highlight_b` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.1)
- **Neon Glow** (`neon_glow`, Light) — `width` [1.0–8.0] (default 3.0)
- **Thermal** (`thermal`, Color) — amount only
- **Night Vision** (`night_vision`, Color) — `noise` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.3), `gain` [0.5–3.0] (default 1.2)
- **Old Film** (`old_film`, Film) — `sepia` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.8), `scratch` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.4), `flicker` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.2)
- **Holographic** (`holographic`, Glitch) — `speed` [0.0–4.0] (default 1.0)
- **RGB Split** (`rgb_split`, Glitch) — `intensity` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.3), `speed` [0.0–4.0] (default 1.0)
- **Posterize** (`posterize`, Color) — `levels` [2.0–16.0] (default 4.0)
- **Halftone** (`halftone`, Pattern) — `size` [2.0–20.0] (default 6.0)
- **Fisheye** (`fisheye`, Warp) — amount only
- **Bleach Bypass** (`bleach_bypass`, Color) — amount only
- **Color Burn** (`color_burn`, Color) — `hue` [0.0–360.0] (default 0.0)
- **CRT** (`crt`, Pattern) — `curvature` [0.05–1.0] (default 0.35), `glow` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.3)
- **Sketch** (`sketch`, Art) — `invert` [0.0–1.0] (default 1.0)
- **Glitch Block** (`glitch_block`, Glitch) — `intensity` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.4), `speed` [0.0–4.0] (default 1.5)
- **Lomo** (`lomo`, Color) — `vignette` [0.1–1.0] (default 0.75), `saturation` [0.8–2.0] (default 1.6), `fade` [0.0–0.5] (default 0.1)
- **Twirl** (`twirl`, Warp) — `radius` [0.1–1.0] (default 0.55)
- **Ripple** (`ripple`, Warp) — `frequency` [2.0–40.0] (default 18.0), `amplitude` [0.005–0.1] (default 0.035), `speed` [0.0–6.0] (default 2.0)
- **Wave Warp** (`wave_warp`, Warp) — `freq_x` [1.0–30.0] (default 8.0), `freq_y` [1.0–30.0] (default 6.0), `amplitude` [0.008–0.12] (default 0.08), `speed` [0.0–5.0] (default 1.5)
- **Breathe** (`breathe`, Motion) — `breathe_rate` [0.1–3.0] (default 0.8), `warp_strength` [0.0–0.15] (default 0.04), `color_speed` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.3), `chroma_split` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.6), `complexity` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.5)
- **Kaleidoscope** (`kaleidoscope`, Warp) — `segments` [2.0–16.0] (default 8.0), `rotation` [0.0–6.28] (default 0.0), `zoom` [0.5–3.0] (default 0.55)
- **Zoom Streak** (`zoom_blur_rad`, Motion) — `intensity` [0.0–0.35] (default 0.12), `cx` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.5), `cy` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.5)
- **Spin Blur** (`spin_blur`, Motion) — `angle` [0.01–0.3] (default 0.08)
- **Heat Haze** (`heat_haze`, Warp) — `intensity` [0.0–2.0] (default 1.8), `speed` [0.0–4.0] (default 1.5)
- **Barrel Warp** (`barrel_warp`, Warp) — `k1` [0.05–1.0] (default 0.4), `k2` [0.0–0.5] (default 0.1), `scale` [0.5–1.0] (default 0.9)
- **Anamorphic Flare** (`anamorphic_streak`, Light) — `threshold` [0.3–1.0] (default 0.7), `length` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.45), `intensity` [0.0–3.0] (default 1.2)
- **Starburst** (`starburst_spike`, Light) — `threshold` [0.3–1.0] (default 0.65), `length` [0.05–0.6] (default 0.25), `rays` [4.0–12.0] (default 6.0)
- **God Rays** (`god_rays`, Light) — `intensity` [0.0–2.0] (default 2.2), `decay` [0.7–1.0] (default 0.94), `cx` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.5), `cy` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.85)
- **Aurora** (`aurora_borealis`, Light) — `intensity` [0.0–2.0] (default 0.7), `speed` [0.0–2.0] (default 0.4), `color_shift` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.3)
- **Bokeh Dream** (`bokeh_dream`, Light) — `radius` [0.0–0.08] (default 0.025), `threshold` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.55), `intensity` [0.0–4.0] (default 1.5)
- **Prism** (`prism_disperse`, Light) — `spread` [0.0–0.2] (default 0.06), `intensity` [0.0–2.0] (default 1.0)
- **Film Burn** (`film_burn`, Film) — `intensity` [0.0–2.0] (default 0.8), `speed` [0.0–3.0] (default 1.2), `edge` [0.0–0.6] (default 0.25)
- **Oil Paint** (`oil_paint`, Art) — `radius` [2.0–8.0] (default 4.0), `sharpness` [0.0–15.0] (default 6.0)
- **Stained Glass** (`stained_glass`, Art) — `cell_size` [4.0–48.0] (default 18.0), `border` [0.0–0.3] (default 0.08), `saturation` [0.5–3.0] (default 1.6)
- **Neon Edges** (`neon_edge_glow`, Light) — `threshold` [0.0–0.5] (default 0.15), `glow` [0.2–2.0] (default 0.8), `hue` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.55)
- **Cyberpunk** (`cyberpunk_grade`, Color) — `shadow_teal` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.9), `hi_orange` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.85), `contrast` [1.0–2.5] (default 1.7)
- **Matrix** (`matrix_rain`, Pattern) — `density` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.45), `speed` [0.5–6.0] (default 2.0), `green_mix` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.55)
- **Pixel Sort** (`pixel_sort`, Glitch) — `threshold` [0.0–0.9] (default 0.35), `intensity` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.7), `direction` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.0)
- **Solarize** (`solarize`, Color) — `threshold` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.5)
- **Double Ghost** (`double_ghost`, Glitch) — `offset` [0.0–0.15] (default 0.06), `opacity` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.65), `angle` [0.0–90.0] (default 10.0)
- **Watercolor** (`watercolor`, Art) — `bleeding` [0.003–0.05] (default 0.018), `paper` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.5), `saturation` [0.8–2.5] (default 1.4)
- **Comic Book** (`comic_dots`, Pattern) — `dot_size` [1.0–10.0] (default 4.0), `ink_threshold` [0.0–0.4] (default 0.12), `color_levels` [2.0–8.0] (default 4.0)
- **Crosshatch** (`crosshatch`, Pattern) — `density` [2.0–20.0] (default 6.0), `thickness` [0.15–0.8] (default 0.5), `angle` [0.0–90.0] (default 0.0)
- **Daguerreotype** (`daguerreotype`, Film) — `tone` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.5), `vignette` [0.1–2.0] (default 0.8), `scratch` [0.02–1.0] (default 0.3)
- **Super 8** (`super8_film`, Film) — `grain` [0.05–1.5] (default 0.5), `gate` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.4), `fade` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.5)
- **VHS Dropout** (`vhs_dropout`, Glitch) — `density` [0.0–0.4] (default 0.12), `speed` [0.0–8.0] (default 3.0)
- **X-Ray** (`x_ray`, Color) — `contrast` [0.5–4.0] (default 1.8), `blue_tint` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.6)
- **Bit Crush** (`bit_crush`, Glitch) — `levels` [2.0–16.0] (default 5.0), `dither` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.4)
- **TV Static** (`tv_static`, Glitch) — `intensity` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.55), `color_mix` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.25)
- **Dither** (`dither_bayer`, Glitch) — `levels` [1.0–8.0] (default 3.0), `scale` [1.0–6.0] (default 2.0), `color` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.4)
- **Miami Vice** (`miami_vice`, Color) — `saturation` [0.5–3.0] (default 1.8)
- **Horror** (`horror_grade`, Color) — `desat` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.8), `red` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.45), `crush` [0.0–0.4] (default 0.15)
- **Split Tone** (`split_toning`, Color) — `shadow_hue` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.6), `hi_hue` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.1)
- **Desert Gold** (`desert_gold`, Color) — `warmth` [0.1–1.0] (default 0.7), `fade` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.6), `haze` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.5)
- **Emboss** (`emboss_relief`, Art) — `angle` [0.0–360.0] (default 135.0), `colorize` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.0)
- **Pointillism** (`pointillist`, Art) — `dot_size` [2.0–14.0] (default 5.0), `scatter` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.4)
- **Interlace** (`interlace_glitch`, Glitch) — `intensity` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.6), `speed` [0.0–6.0] (default 2.0)
- **Frosted Glass** (`frosted_glass`, Warp) — `blur` [0.003–0.05] (default 0.018), `noise` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.4), `tint` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.3)
- **Mirror Fold** (`mirror_fold`, Warp) — `axis` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.5), `vertical` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.0)
- **Echo Trails** (`echo_trails`, Motion) — `offset` [0.005–0.1] (default 0.025), `fade` [0.1–0.9] (default 0.55), `angle` [0.0–360.0] (default 45.0)
- **Gradient Map** (`gradient_map`, Color) — `hue1` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.65), `hue2` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.12)
- **Cross Process** (`cross_process`, Color) — `contrast` [1.0–2.5] (default 1.4)
- **Plasma** (`plasma_field`, Pattern) — `scale` [1.0–12.0] (default 4.0), `speed` [0.0–5.0] (default 1.5), `intensity` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.55)
- **Fire Edge** (`fire_edge`, Light) — `intensity` [0.0–2.5] (default 1.5), `speed` [0.0–5.0] (default 2.0), `height` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.25)
- **Laser Grid** (`laser_grid`, Pattern) — `grid_size` [4.0–40.0] (default 14.0), `hue` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.5), `intensity` [0.0–2.0] (default 0.8)
- **Technicolor** (`technicolor`, Color) — `saturation` [1.0–4.0] (default 2.0), `contrast` [1.0–2.5] (default 1.3), `warmth` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.35)
- **Ice Crystal** (`ice_crystal`, Light) — `scale` [2.0–20.0] (default 8.0), `refract` [0.01–0.15] (default 0.04), `tint` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.35)
- **Kodachrome** (`kodachrome`, Color) — `saturation` [0.8–3.0] (default 1.5), `reds` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.5), `shadows` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.4)
- **Vortex** (`vortex_distort`, Warp) — `scale` [1.0–8.0] (default 3.0), `speed` [0.0–3.0] (default 0.8)
- **Sepia** (`sepia_rich`, Color) — `vignette` [0.0–2.0] (default 0.7), `contrast` [0.5–2.0] (default 1.2)
- **Digital Noise** (`digital_noise`, Glitch) — `intensity` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.5), `color_sep` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.7), `luma_bias` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.5)
- **Color Dodge** (`color_dodge`, Color) — `intensity` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.5), `hue` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.08), `glow` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.6)
- **Pop Art** (`warhol_pop`, Art) — `levels` [2.0–8.0] (default 4.0), `hue_shift` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.25), `saturation` [1.0–4.0] (default 2.5)
- **Infrared** (`infrared_film`, Color) — `channel_mix` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.8), `glow` [0.05–1.0] (default 0.4), `contrast` [0.5–3.0] (default 1.4)
- **RGB Scanlines** (`scanline_color`, Glitch) — `line_width` [1.0–6.0] (default 3.0), `intensity` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.7), `rgb_sep` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.5)
- **Data Corrupt** (`data_corrupt`, Glitch) — `density` [0.0–0.5] (default 0.18), `block_size` [2.0–24.0] (default 8.0), `intensity` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.9)
- **Long Exposure** (`long_exposure`, Motion) — `threshold` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.35), `trail` [0.01–0.2] (default 0.06), `glow` [0.1–2.0] (default 1.0)
- **Pixel Mosaic** (`pixel_mosaic`, Glitch) — `block_size` [2.0–64.0] (default 16.0), `color_steps` [2.0–16.0] (default 6.0)
- **Thermal Camera** (`thermal_map`, Color) — `cold_hue` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.65), `hot_hue` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.08), `contrast` [0.8–3.0] (default 1.4), `scanlines` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.15)
- **Tilt-Shift Miniature** (`tilt_shift`, Warp) — `focus_y` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.5), `focus_band` [0.02–0.5] (default 0.08), `blur_radius` [2.0–30.0] (default 12.0), `saturation` [1.0–3.0] (default 1.8)
- **Raindrops** (`raindrop_refract`, Warp) — `density` [1.0–20.0] (default 8.0), `size` [0.1–1.0] (default 0.45), `refract_str` [0.1–2.0] (default 1.2)
- **Risograph Print** (`risograph`, Art) — `hue1` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.58), `hue2` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.02), `dot_size` [1.0–8.0] (default 3.0), `misreg` [0.0–0.02] (default 0.004), `paper` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.9)
- **Vintage Negative** (`vintage_negative`, Film) — `orange_mask` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.55), `contrast` [0.5–2.5] (default 1.3), `grain` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.12)
- **Pencil Sketch** (`pencil_sketch`, Art) — `line_str` [0.5–5.0] (default 2.5), `paper_tone` [0.7–1.0] (default 0.95), `hatching` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.4)
- **CRT Barrel** (`crt_barrel`, Pattern) — `distort` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.35), `corner_dark` [0.0–3.0] (default 1.8), `rgb_shift` [0.0–0.02] (default 0.004), `scanline` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.35)
- **RGB Wave Split** (`rgb_split_wave`, Glitch) — `amplitude` [0.003–0.05] (default 0.018), `frequency` [1.0–20.0] (default 6.0), `speed` [0.0–10.0] (default 2.5)
- **Golden Hour** (`golden_hour`, Color) — `warmth` [0.1–1.0] (default 0.8), `glow_str` [0.1–1.0] (default 0.7), `shadow_lift` [0.0–0.3] (default 0.15), `vignette` [0.1–2.0] (default 0.5)
- **Neon Sign** (`neon_sign`, Light) — `edge_str` [1.0–10.0] (default 4.0), `glow_radius` [2.0–20.0] (default 8.0), `hue_shift` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.0), `bg_darken` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.75)
- **Mirror Tunnel** (`mirror_tunnel`, Warp) — `depth` [2.0–12.0] (default 5.0), `rotation` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.12), `zoom` [0.3–0.95] (default 0.65)
- **Liquid Chrome** (`liquid_chrome`, Warp) — `flow` [0.1–1.0] (default 0.5), `metallic` [0.1–1.0] (default 0.7), `tint_r` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.85), `tint_g` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.9), `tint_b` [0.0–1.0] (default 1.0)
- **Zone System B&W** (`zone_system_bw`, Color) — `zones` [3.0–10.0] (default 6.0), `contrast` [0.8–3.0] (default 1.6), `grain` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.08), `paper_white` [0.85–1.0] (default 0.96)
- **Glitter Dust** (`glitter_dust`, Light) — `density` [100.0–2000.0] (default 800.0), `size` [0.2–2.0] (default 0.8), `sparkle` [0.2–2.0] (default 1.0), `color_var` [0.0–1.0] (default 1.0)
- **Contour Map** (`contour_map`, Pattern) — `levels` [3.0–20.0] (default 8.0), `line_width` [0.01–0.15] (default 0.04), `line_hue` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.35), `fill_sat` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.6)
- **Film Halation** (`film_halation`, Film) — `threshold` [0.3–0.9] (default 0.3), `radius` [2.0–20.0] (default 16.0), `red_shift` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.75)
- **ASCII Art** (`ascii_art`, Pattern) — `char_size` [4.0–24.0] (default 10.0), `fg_r` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.0), `fg_g` [0.0–1.0] (default 1.0), `fg_b` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.15), `bg_dark` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.85)
- **DNA Helix Grid** (`dna_helix`, Pattern) — `grid_scale` [5.0–30.0] (default 12.0), `wave_amp` [0.15–1.0] (default 0.5), `line_width` [0.02–0.15] (default 0.05), `hue` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.55), `bg_darken` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.6)
- **Double Exposure** (`double_exposure`, Film) — `offset_x` [-0.3–0.3] (default 0.15), `offset_y` [-0.3–0.3] (default -0.1), `scale2` [0.5–2.0] (default 1.15), `desaturate2` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.6), `opacity` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.8)
- **Ken Burns** (`ken_burns`, Motion) — `start_scale` [0.5–4.0] (default 1.0), `end_scale` [0.5–4.0] (default 1.3), `start_x` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.5), `start_y` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.5), `end_x` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.5), `end_y` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.5)
- **Skin Smooth** (`skin_smooth`, Beauty) — `radius` [1.0–6.0] (default 3.0), `tone` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.5)
- **Glow Up** (`glow_up`, Beauty) — `glow` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.5), `warmth` [-1.0–1.0] (default 0.25), `brighten` [0.0–0.5] (default 0.15)
- **Cam Shake** (`cam_shake`, Motion) — `intensity` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.5), `speed` [0.1–4.0] (default 1.0)
- **Chroma Key** (`greenscreen`, Tools) — `key_r` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.0), `key_g` [0.0–1.0] (default 1.0), `key_b` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.0), `similarity` [0.01–1.0] (default 0.4), `smoothness` [0.0–0.5] (default 0.08), `spill` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.5)
- **Retro Beauty** (`retro_beauty`, Beauty) — `glow` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.6), `fade` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.5), `blush` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.35)
- **2016** (`insta_2016`, Color) — `fade` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.45), `pop` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.6), `warmth` [-1.0–1.0] (default 0.35)
- **Glass Skin** (`glass_skin`, Beauty) — `radius` [1.0–6.0] (default 3.5), `gloss` [0.0–1.0] (default 0.5)

## Engine lifecycle (not agent tools — C ABI, see pms_engine.h)

- `pms_create / pms_destroy` — engine instance
- `pms_tick(dt)` — advance timers, pump workers
- `pms_render(target_texture, w, h)` — composite the current frame
- `pms_command(json) -> json` — every lever above
- `pms_poll_events() -> json[]` — progress, meters, tracker state, take events
- `pms_submit_camera_frame / pms_submit_mic_block` — capture intake
- `pms_set_asset_root / pms_model_status` — bundle + downloaded model packs
